mnemonics: PACKAGE PROTECTED: Public Outside, Private Inside. Default is Package-Family Only.
keyPoints: A package is a grouping of related classes and interfaces.
Member access is controlled by access modifiers: public, protected, (default), and private.
A class can access members of another class in the same package with default (package-private) access, but a class in a different package cannot.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to import a package and then using the fully qualified class name, leading to verbose and hard-to-read code.
Assuming a subclass in a different package can access a superclass member with default (package-private) access; it cannot, it needs at least protected access.
